name: Jyotish — Canonical
description: A canonical Vedic astrology (Jyotish) grammar covering grahas (planets), rashis (signs), bhavas (houses), and nakshatras (lunar mansions). A living document — contribute at github.com/PlayfulProcess/recursive.eco-schemas

sections.Story: Surya is the Atma — the soul, the divine light of consciousness. In Jyotish, the Sun represents the father, authority, government, and the eternal self that is beyond birth and death.
sections.Light: Authority, dharma, self-realization, leadership, vitality, honor from the state.
sections.Shadow: Excessive pride, domination, father wounds, separation from community.
sections.Dignity: Exalted in Mesha (Aries). Debilitated in Tula (Libra). Own sign: Simha (Leo).

sections.Story: Chandra is Manas — the mind, the feeling nature, the flow of consciousness moment to moment. In Jyotish, the Moon is arguably more important than the Sun; the Rashi chart is calculated from the Moon sign.
sections.Light: Emotional intelligence, nurturing, public connection, fertility, comfort.
sections.Shadow: Instability of mind, emotional dependency, anxiety, attachment to comfort.
sections.Dignity: Exalted in Vrishabha (Taurus). Debilitated in Vrishchika (Scorpio). Own sign: Karka (Cancer).

sections.Story: Mangala is the commander — raw energy, courage, and the power to act. Mars in Jyotish governs brothers, property, surgery, and the fire of tapas (spiritual heat).
sections.Light: Courage, technical skill, logical precision, protective strength, land ownership.
sections.Shadow: Anger, accidents, conflicts with siblings, Mangal Dosha in marriage.
sections.Dignity: Exalted in Makara (Capricorn). Debilitated in Karka (Cancer). Own signs: Mesha (Aries), Vrishchika (Scorpio).

sections.Story: Budha is discrimination — the intellect that sorts, categorizes, and communicates. Mercury in Jyotish is the prince, adaptable and influenced by the planets it associates with.
sections.Light: Intelligence, speech, commerce, mathematics, writing, humor.
sections.Shadow: Nervous disorders, duplicity, overthinking, skin conditions.
sections.Dignity: Exalted in Kanya (Virgo). Debilitated in Meena (Pisces). Own signs: Mithuna (Gemini), Kanya (Virgo).

sections.Story: Guru (Brihaspati) is the great teacher — wisdom, dharma, and divine grace. Jupiter in Jyotish is the most benefic planet, governing children, husband (in a woman's chart), wealth, and spiritual knowledge.
sections.Light: Wisdom, prosperity, children, spiritual teaching, dharma, expansion.
sections.Shadow: Overindulgence, false teaching, weight gain, complacency, dogmatism.
sections.Dignity: Exalted in Karka (Cancer). Debilitated in Makara (Capricorn). Own signs: Dhanus (Sagittarius), Meena (Pisces).

sections.Story: Shukra is the guru of the asuras — beauty, desire, and the knowledge of resurrection. Venus in Jyotish governs wife (in a man's chart), luxury, vehicles, poetry, and the arts.
sections.Light: Beauty, romance, luxury, artistic talent, vehicles, diplomacy.
sections.Shadow: Lust, over-attachment to pleasure, vanity, materialism.
sections.Dignity: Exalted in Meena (Pisces). Debilitated in Kanya (Virgo). Own signs: Vrishabha (Taurus), Tula (Libra).

sections.Story: Shani is the great karmic teacher — slow, heavy, and exacting. Saturn in Jyotish governs suffering, service, longevity, discipline, and the working class. Sade Sati (7.5-year Saturn transit) is a rite of passage.
sections.Light: Discipline, longevity, service, detachment, spiritual maturity, democratic values.
sections.Shadow: Chronic illness, delay, depression, poverty, isolation, fear.
sections.Dignity: Exalted in Tula (Libra). Debilitated in Mesha (Aries). Own signs: Makara (Capricorn), Kumbha (Aquarius).

sections.Story: Rahu is the shadow planet of obsession — the head of the serpent who swallowed the nectar of immortality. It represents insatiable desire, foreign influences, technology, and unconventional paths.
sections.Light: Innovation, foreign connections, research, material ambition, breaking taboos.
sections.Shadow: Obsession, illusion, addiction, scandals, fear of the unknown.
sections.Dignity: Strong in Vrishabha/Mithuna. Weak in Vrishchika/Dhanus. Acts like the lord of the sign it occupies.

sections.Story: Ketu is the shadow planet of liberation — the tail of the serpent, representing spiritual detachment, past-life mastery, and the impulse to transcend the material world.
sections.Light: Spiritual insight, moksha, past-life skills, healing, mysticism.
sections.Shadow: Confusion, self-doubt, isolation, psychic disturbances, loss.
sections.Dignity: Strong in Vrishchika/Dhanus. Weak in Vrishabha/Mithuna. Acts like the lord of the sign it occupies.

sections.Story: Mesha is the first impulse — pure initiating fire. Ruled by Mangala, this is where the soul first takes action. Pitta constitution, cardinal fire, the warrior's beginning.
sections.Light: Courage, independence, pioneering spirit, athletic ability.
sections.Shadow: Rashness, selfishness, burns and fevers, head injuries.

sections.Story: Vrishabha is the fertile earth — wealth, beauty, and sensory pleasure. Ruled by Shukra, this sign builds, stabilizes, and attracts. The bull who does not move unless moved by love.
sections.Light: Stability, wealth accumulation, artistic sense, loyalty.
sections.Shadow: Stubbornness, materialism, throat ailments, possessiveness.

sections.Story: Mithuna is the twins — duality, communication, and the play of the mind. Ruled by Budha, this sign gathers, connects, and distributes knowledge.
sections.Light: Communication, versatility, business acumen, humor.
sections.Shadow: Restlessness, duplicity, nervous exhaustion, skin issues.

sections.Story: Karka is the crab — the protective shell around tender feeling. Ruled by Chandra, this is where home, mother, and emotional nourishment reside. Guru (Jupiter) finds exaltation here.
sections.Light: Emotional depth, nurturing, home-making, public influence.
sections.Shadow: Moodiness, clinging, digestive issues, over-protectiveness.

sections.Story: Simha is the lion — sovereignty, creative power, and the radiance of the soul. Ruled by Surya, this is where dharma shines through personal expression and leadership.
sections.Light: Leadership, creative power, generosity, dignity, charisma.
sections.Shadow: Pride, autocracy, heart conditions, need for adulation.

sections.Story: Kanya is the maiden — discrimination, purity, and service. Ruled by Budha, this is where intellect meets devotion. The sign of the artisan, the healer, and the analyst.
sections.Light: Analytical skill, service orientation, health awareness, craftsmanship.
sections.Shadow: Criticism, worry, intestinal disorders, perfectionism.

sections.Story: Tula is the scales — balance, justice, and the art of relationship. Ruled by Shukra, this is where Shani (Saturn) finds exaltation. Commerce, law, and partnership thrive here.
sections.Light: Diplomacy, fairness, aesthetic sense, commercial skill.
sections.Shadow: Indecision, dependency, kidney/lower back issues, superficiality.

sections.Story: Vrishchika is the scorpion — transformation, hidden power, and the occult. Ruled by Mangala (and Ketu), this is the sign of deep research, mystery, and regeneration.
sections.Light: Research ability, occult knowledge, psychological depth, resilience.
sections.Shadow: Jealousy, secrecy, reproductive issues, vindictiveness.

sections.Story: Dhanus is the archer — dharma, higher learning, and the search for truth. Ruled by Guru, this is the sign of the teacher, the philosopher, and the seeker who fires arrows at the infinite.
sections.Light: Wisdom, teaching ability, righteousness, long-distance travel.
sections.Shadow: Dogmatism, hip/thigh issues, excess, preachiness.

sections.Story: Makara is the sea-goat — ambition, structure, and the climb to worldly mastery. Ruled by Shani, this is where Mangala (Mars) finds exaltation. Karma yoga in its purest form.
sections.Light: Ambition, organizational skill, endurance, practical wisdom.
sections.Shadow: Coldness, workaholism, bone/joint issues, pessimism.

sections.Story: Kumbha is the water-bearer — humanitarian vision, networks, and gains. Ruled by Shani (and Rahu), this sign serves the collective through innovation and detachment.
sections.Light: Humanitarian ideals, scientific thinking, networking, gains from groups.
sections.Shadow: Eccentric detachment, circulation issues, aloofness.

sections.Story: Meena is the two fish — dissolution, moksha, and the ocean of consciousness. Ruled by Guru, this is where Shukra (Venus) finds exaltation. The final sign, where all rivers return to the sea.
sections.Light: Spiritual depth, compassion, imagination, final liberation.
sections.Shadow: Escapism, foot ailments, confusion, loss of boundaries.

sections.Story: The horsemen healers — twin physicians of the gods. Speed, healing, and the miracle of new beginnings. 0°–13°20' Mesha.
sections.Light: Healing ability, speed, youthful energy, pioneering medicine.
sections.Shadow: Impulsiveness, superficial healing, recklessness.

sections.Story: The bearer — the yoni, the womb of creation and destruction. Bharani holds the power of restraint and transformation. 13°20'–26°40' Mesha.
sections.Light: Creative power, endurance, nurturing through difficulty, sexual vitality.
sections.Shadow: Jealousy, moral rigidity, struggles with sexuality.

sections.Story: The razor — the fire that cuts and purifies. Krittika spans the border of Mesha and Vrishabha, the foster mothers of Kartikeya. 26°40' Mesha – 10° Vrishabha.
sections.Light: Purification, courage, clarity, strong digestion, truthfulness.
sections.Shadow: Harshness, destructive criticism, inflammatory conditions.

sections.Story: The red one — fertility, beauty, and the creative power of desire. Rohini was the Moon's favorite wife. 10°–23°20' Vrishabha.
sections.Light: Beauty, artistic talent, magnetism, fertility, material abundance.
sections.Shadow: Possessiveness, jealousy, over-attachment to beauty.

sections.Story: The deer's head — the eternal seeker, always searching, always curious. Mrigashira spans Vrishabha–Mithuna. 23°20' Vrishabha – 6°40' Mithuna.
sections.Light: Seeking, research, gentle nature, perceptiveness, travel.
sections.Shadow: Fickleness, never settling, suspicious nature.

sections.Story: The teardrop — the storm that clears, the destruction that renews. Ardra is Rudra's star, fierce and transformative. 6°40'–20° Mithuna.
sections.Light: Intellectual brilliance, research, effort, transformation through crisis.
sections.Shadow: Destructiveness, cruelty, storms of emotion, arrogance.

sections.Story: Return of the light — renewal, restoration, and the journey home. Punarvasu spans Mithuna–Karka. 20° Mithuna – 3°20' Karka.
sections.Light: Renewal, philosophical understanding, contentment, return to wholeness.
sections.Shadow: Restlessness, over-simplification, lack of ambition.

sections.Story: The nourisher — the most auspicious nakshatra, where Brihaspati's blessings flow. 3°20'–16°40' Karka.
sections.Light: Nourishment, generosity, spiritual growth, protection, prosperity.
sections.Shadow: Over-attachment, orthodoxy, emotional manipulation.

sections.Story: The serpent's embrace — kundalini energy, hypnotic power, and the wisdom of the nagas. 16°40'–30° Karka.
sections.Light: Kundalini awakening, mystical insight, political cunning, healing through venom.
sections.Shadow: Deception, cold-bloodedness, poison, manipulation.
